current.summary: Deep research on Agent Skills spec community sentiment, then adversarial review of metadata convention provenance design, leading to v0.1 strategy decisions

activeWork.description: Agent Skills Metadata Convention v0.1 — preparing for community review and publication
activeWork.progress: Completed: (1) Deep community sentiment research across GitHub, Reddit, HN, blogs, Twitter — 15 research files written to docs/research/agent-skills-spec/. (2) Mapped all existing solution proposals for top 5 pain points. (3) Discovered spec has ZERO community contributions in 3.5 months. (4) Ran 6-persona adversarial review of full metadata convention. (5) Ran 6-persona deep dive specifically on provenance. (6) Made two key strategy decisions: v0.1 ships with complete trust story (signed attestation, not unsigned), and outreach starts with agent ecosystem folks.
blockers: Need to resolve content_hash edge cases (CRLF/binary, blanking regex, symlinks, unicode normalization) before v0.1
Need to decide signing mechanism (SSH vs Sigstore) and implement it
Need real human review from agent ecosystem community members

git.lastCommit: 887467d docs: 5 ADRs for content discovery redesign architectural decisions

recentDecisions: v0.1 ships with COMPLETE trust story — signed attestation included, not deferred to v0.2. Rationale: 'half-baked security means nobody will care'
Outreach starts with agent ecosystem community members, then expands to registry maintainers and spec authors
Metadata convention uses sidecar file pattern (SKILL.meta.yaml) with single metadata_file field in SKILL.md — validated by 6 adversarial personas
Content hash needs fixes before v0.1: drop CRLF normalization (corrupts binaries), specify exact blanking regex, define symlink behavior, add unicode normalization rules
Need reference implementation + test vectors for content_hash — non-negotiable for 33+ tools to agree on hashes

Community member feedback: 'spec stuff should not be defined by Claude — carefully considered by a human.' Response: LLM is for research and pressure-testing, human makes final design decisions, real community review before publication
nextSteps: Fix content_hash edge cases identified by panel (CRLF/binary, blanking regex, symlinks, unicode) in metadata_convention.md
Evaluate SSH vs Sigstore for attestation signing — make decision and add to v0.1 scope
Build reference implementation for content_hash with 20+ test vectors
Prepare metadata convention for agent ecosystem community review — distill to a presentation that's honest about LLM-assisted research + human design decisions
Reach out to agent ecosystem folks for real human review of provenance section
Commit all research files (16 new files in docs/research/agent-skills-spec/)

context.notes: This session was entirely research and strategy — no code changes. 16 research files written covering: community sentiment (5 source types), solution proposals for all 5 pain points, spec governance history, 6-persona adversarial review of full convention, and 6-persona provenance deep dive. Key insight: the Agent Skills spec has accepted ZERO community contributions in 3.5 months (48 proposals filed, 1 answered). The metadata convention is well-positioned as a community-led complement, not a fork. The adversarial panel converged on 8 minimum fields (provenance + triggers) but Holden decided to ship with complete trust story including signing. A community member cautioned against LLM-defined specs — valid feedback that should shape how we present the convention (LLM as research tool, human as decision-maker).
